summary: This source consists of brief show notes from an episode of the Dwarkesh Podcast featuring Andrej Karpathy. It outlines his high-level views on AGI timelines, the economic impact of AGI, and the state of reinforcement learning, but lacks detailed transcripts or direct quotes.

principles.statement: AGI is still a decade away and will not cause an immediate, unprecedented economic explosion.
principles.rationale: He believes AGI's economic impact will simply blend into the previous ~2.5 centuries of 2% GDP growth rather than causing a sudden spike.

anti_patterns.description: Viewing reinforcement learning as an elegant or ideal solution.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: Karpathy considers reinforcement learning to be 'terrible,' though he concedes that all other current alternatives are much worse.
